text: A tensile test measures the stress required to elongate a metal specimen. The initial linear slope gives Young's modulus, while permanent deformation begins after the yield point.
Dislocations move through a crystal under shear stress. Grain boundaries obstruct that motion, so reducing grain size can increase yield strength through the Hall-Petch effect.
A face-centered cubic lattice has atoms at each corner and face center of the unit cell. Aluminum, copper, and nickel commonly adopt this crystal structure.
During annealing, recovery reduces stored strain energy before recrystallization produces new low-defect grains. Subsequent grain growth is driven by a reduction in grain-boundary area.
A polymer above its glass-transition temperature gains segmental mobility and becomes more compliant. The transition is kinetic and differs from a sharp thermodynamic melting point.
Carbon-fiber composites carry load primarily along the fiber direction. Their stiffness is anisotropic, and weak interfaces can lead to delamination under out-of-plane loading.
In a double-slit experiment, coherent waves interfere to form alternating bright and dark fringes. Measuring which path a particle takes destroys the phase relation required for that pattern.
The entropy of an isolated system tends to increase because macrostates with many compatible microstates are overwhelmingly more probable than specially ordered configurations.
A changing magnetic flux induces an electromotive force in a conducting loop. Lenz's law determines the direction of the induced current so that it opposes the flux change.
Special relativity combines space and time into spacetime. Observers in relative motion can disagree about elapsed time and measured length while agreeing on the spacetime interval.
A harmonic oscillator experiences a restoring force proportional to displacement. With weak damping, its amplitude decays gradually while the system continues to oscillate near its natural frequency.
The band gap separates occupied valence states from empty conduction states in a semiconductor. Thermal excitation or doping can create mobile electrons and holes that carry current.

At chemical equilibrium, forward and reverse reaction rates are equal even though molecules continue reacting. Changing concentration shifts the equilibrium composition according to Le Chatelier's principle.
A catalyst lowers the activation barrier by providing an alternative reaction pathway. It accelerates both directions of a reversible reaction without changing the equilibrium constant.
An acid donates a proton and a Brønsted base accepts one. A buffer resists changes in pH because a weak acid and its conjugate base consume added base or acid.
Nuclear magnetic resonance distinguishes chemical environments through resonance frequencies and spin-spin splitting. Peak integration can estimate the relative number of contributing nuclei.
An oxidation reaction transfers electrons from a reducing agent to an oxidizing agent. The paired reduction half-reaction gains the electrons lost by the oxidation half-reaction.
Molecular orbitals form from linear combinations of atomic orbitals. Bonding orbitals concentrate electron density between nuclei, whereas antibonding orbitals introduce a node between them.
DNA polymerase extends a new strand in the five-prime to three-prime direction using the complementary template. Proofreading removes many incorrectly incorporated nucleotides.

Natural selection changes allele frequencies when heritable traits affect reproductive success. Adaptation describes a population-level evolutionary outcome rather than an individual's deliberate response.
A neuron integrates excitatory and inhibitory synaptic inputs. When membrane voltage crosses threshold, voltage-gated channels generate an action potential that propagates along the axon.

Enzyme kinetics often follow a saturating relationship between substrate concentration and reaction velocity. At high substrate concentration, most active sites are occupied and velocity approaches a maximum.
The innate immune system responds rapidly through conserved pattern-recognition receptors. Adaptive immunity develops antigen-specific B-cell and T-cell responses with immunological memory.
Photosynthesis couples light-driven electron transport to carbon fixation. The proton gradient across the thylakoid membrane powers ATP synthase, while NADPH supplies reducing power.
A finite-element model approximates a continuous field with basis functions defined on a mesh. Refining the mesh can reduce discretization error but increases the linear system size.
Gradient descent updates parameters opposite the loss gradient. A learning rate that is too large can destabilize optimization, while a very small rate can make convergence impractically slow.
Monte Carlo integration estimates an expectation using random samples. Its standard error usually decreases in proportion to the inverse square root of the sample count.
Cross-validation estimates predictive performance by repeatedly separating training and validation observations. Preprocessing must be fit inside each training fold to prevent information leakage.
A sparse matrix stores only nonzero entries and their indices. Iterative solvers can exploit that structure to analyze systems that would be too large for a dense factorization.
Bayesian inference combines a prior distribution with a likelihood to obtain a posterior distribution. Posterior predictive checks compare simulated observations with the measured data.
A randomized controlled experiment assigns treatments independently of measured and unmeasured covariates. Attrition and noncompliance can still complicate interpretation of the estimated effect.
A confidence interval describes uncertainty under repeated sampling assumptions. It should not be interpreted as the posterior probability that a fixed parameter lies inside the observed interval.
Correlation alone does not identify a causal effect because confounding variables can influence both measured quantities. A causal design requires explicit assumptions and suitable controls.

A preregistered analysis specifies hypotheses, exclusion rules, outcomes, and statistical models before inspecting the results. Exploratory findings remain useful when they are reported transparently as exploratory.
Measurement error can attenuate an estimated association and distort uncertainty. Calibration samples and repeated measurements help characterize precision, bias, and detection limits.
Replication tests whether an effect generalizes across samples, laboratories, and reasonable analysis choices. Repeating the same biased procedure with the same data does not establish robustness.
The restaurant adjusted its dinner menu after customers requested more vegetarian choices. The chef added a roasted vegetable dish and changed the soup recipe.
A traveler compared train schedules before reserving a hotel near the central station. The morning route was cheaper, but the afternoon route required fewer transfers.
The bank approved a fixed-rate loan after reviewing the applicant's income and credit history. Monthly payments will remain constant unless taxes or insurance costs change.
During the football match, the home team defended a narrow lead and substituted a midfielder. A late corner kick produced the final goal.
The museum opened a new exhibition of landscape paintings and handwritten travel journals. Visitors can reserve timed tickets through the museum website.
A city council debated the annual transportation budget. Residents asked for safer crossings, more frequent buses, and repairs to several neighborhood streets.
The baker folded chilled butter into the dough, rested it overnight, and shaped the pastries before sunrise. The first batch emerged crisp and evenly browned.
A novelist revised the opening chapter to clarify the narrator's motivation. The editor suggested shortening two scenes and moving a revelation closer to the ending.
Crack growth can accelerate when cyclic loading concentrates stress at a flaw tip. Fatigue life depends on stress range, geometry, environment, and the material's resistance to propagation.
Diffusion becomes faster as temperature rises because more atoms overcome migration barriers. Substitutional diffusion generally proceeds through vacancies in a crystalline solid.
Conservation of angular momentum makes a rotating skater spin faster when mass moves closer to the rotation axis, provided the external torque remains negligible.
A wave packet contains a range of frequencies. Dispersion causes those frequency components to propagate at different velocities, changing the packet shape over time.

Chromatography separates compounds because they partition differently between stationary and mobile phases. Retention time alone is not definitive identification without standards or complementary measurements.
Increasing temperature can favor an endothermic equilibrium direction because heat acts like a reactant. The rate constants may also increase, which is distinct from the equilibrium shift.
Ribosomes translate messenger RNA codons into a polypeptide sequence. Transfer RNAs pair anticodons with codons while carrying the corresponding amino acids.
Genetic drift produces random allele-frequency changes, with stronger effects in small populations. It can remove a beneficial allele even when natural selection favors that allele.
Numerical differentiation amplifies measurement noise because it subtracts nearby values. Smoothing or fitting a differentiable model can trade variance against bias.
A held-out test set should remain untouched during model selection. Repeatedly choosing models based on test performance turns the test set into part of the optimization process.
A statistically significant result can have negligible practical importance when the sample is very large. Effect sizes and uncertainty intervals are needed to judge scientific relevance.
Negative controls can reveal contamination, batch effects, or unintended pathways. A control is informative only when its expected response follows from the experimental design.
The library extended weekend hours during the examination period. Students may book group rooms online, while quiet floors remain available without reservations.
A family planted herbs and tomatoes in containers on the balcony. Regular watering kept the soil moist during an unusually warm week.
The company postponed its product launch after a supplier missed the delivery deadline. Managers informed customers and offered refunds for advance orders.
A photographer arrived before sunset to choose a viewpoint above the harbor. Clouds reflected orange light while boats returned to the marina.
